snippets.title: Exact GELU with the error function
snippets.explanation: Φ(x) = ½(1 + erf(x/√2)). This is the mathematically exact form; libraries may use approximations for speed.

snippets.title: PyTorch GELU in an MLP block
snippets.explanation: Standard BERT-style FFN: expand → GELU → project back. Residual connections wrap this block in full Transformer layers.

snippets.title: Compare GELU and ReLU on the same tensor
snippets.code:
import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F

x = torch.linspace(-3, 3, 7)
gelu = F.gelu(x)
relu = torch.relu(x)
print(torch.stack([x, gelu, relu], dim=1))
snippets.explanation: For negative x, ReLU is exactly 0 while GELU is small and negative; for large positive x, both track x closely.
